fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency responder access and movement at the property could be supported by creating additional access points, which may allow responders to reach the main building more efficiently. Expanding the current single access point or establishing secondary entryways may improve access speed. Consideration for widening or optimizing the front driveway could facilitate the movement of emergency vehicles, such as fire engines and ambulances, ensuring they can navigate around the property readily. Enhancing visibility and accessibility at potential staging areas, such as vehicle parking zones, would further support effective emergency operations. Moreover, maintaining clear access next to landscaped areas can assist in preventing any impediments to quick response maneuvers. Improving access routes on the property could help streamline emergency operations and ensure personnel can conduct their duties promptly and effectively.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by managing vegetation directly adjacent to the structure and strategically reducing the vegetated perimeter could decrease fuel continuity and help manage fire exposure. Reducing the density of the tree cluster and maintaining separation between scattered trees may improve defensible space around the property. Managing the dense tree canopy to reduce fuel concentrations near improvements could further support this goal. Additionally, increasing clearance between vegetation and the fence could reduce potential fire spread to the structure. Improving rear access by managing vegetation could enhance firefighter access and suppression efforts. Addressing the vegetated access route by thinning or trimming vegetation could also aid in improving safe and efficient firefighter ingress and egress while supporting long-term property preparedness.
